description: Vibemux UX improvements: fix prefix key conflict (Ctrl+B default), add top titlebar with GUI buttons, first-launch onboarding wizard, and context menus/buttons on Deck panes and Shelf cards for full mouse operability.

userStories.title: Change default Prefix Key to Ctrl+B and make it config-driven
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want the default Prefix Key to not conflict with Spotlight so that I can use Navigation Mode without issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Rust KeysConfig default value changed from 'cmd+space' to 'ctrl+b' in config.rs
New src/lib/keymap.ts with parsePrefixKey(configString) function that parses 'ctrl+b', 'cmd+space', 'ctrl+`', 'ctrl+a' etc. into { ctrlKey, metaKey, altKey, shiftKey, key } matcher object
parsePrefixKey handles: ctrl, cmd (maps to metaKey on macOS, ctrlKey elsewhere), alt, shift modifiers + any single key
parsePrefixKey handles backtick key: 'ctrl+`' matches e.key === '`' or e.code === 'Backquote'
App.svelte loads keys.prefix from config_get on mount and uses parsePrefixKey to dynamically bind the prefix key instead of hardcoded Cmd+Space / Ctrl+Space check
Nav Mode indicator bar displays the actual configured prefix key (e.g. 'Ctrl+B') instead of hardcoded text
HelpOverlay displays the actual configured prefix key via a prefixKey prop that reflects the real config value
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add top Titlebar with New Session, Search, and Settings buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want a toolbar at the top of the window with common action buttons so that I can operate without relying on keyboard shortcuts.
acceptanceCriteria: New Titlebar.svelte component fixed at the top of the window
Titlebar height is 36px, background matches app theme (#111111 or slightly lighter), 1px bottom border separator
Left side shows current Workspace identifier: color dot + number (e.g. a colored circle + '1 Default'), clicking it allows inline rename
Right side button group (left to right): + button (New Session), magnifying glass button (Search), gear button (Settings)
Each button shows tooltip on hover with function name and keyboard shortcut (e.g. 'New Session (Ctrl+B, N)')
Tooltips display the actual configured prefix key from config, not hardcoded
Remove the existing floating gear button (settings-btn) from App.svelte bottom-right corner
App.svelte integrates Titlebar: Deck area height = 100vh minus titlebar height minus shelf height (if shelf visible)
Titlebar z-index prevents terminal content from overlapping it
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add onboarding_completed config field and detect_shells Tauri command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the backend support for onboarding (config field + shell detection) so that the frontend onboarding wizard has the data it needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add onboarding_completed: bool field to UserConfig in config.rs with #[serde(default)] defaulting to false
Existing configs without onboarding_completed field load successfully (serde default kicks in)
config_get returns onboarding_completed in the JSON response
config_update accepts onboarding_completed field and persists it
New detect_shells Tauri command: on macOS/Linux reads /etc/shells, filters to paths that exist on disk, returns Vec<String>
detect_shells on Windows checks for powershell.exe, pwsh.exe, cmd.exe existence and returns found paths
detect_shells is registered in the invoke_handler in lib.rs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build Onboarding wizard — Prefix Key selection (Step 1)
userStories.description: As a first-time Vibemux user, I want to choose my preferred Prefix Key on first launch so that keyboard shortcuts don't conflict with my habits.
acceptanceCriteria: New Onboarding.svelte component renders as a full-screen overlay
App.svelte checks onboarding_completed from config_get on mount; if false, shows Onboarding overlay instead of creating a session
Step 1 title: 'Choose your Navigation Key'
Explanation text: 'Press this key combo to enter Navigation Mode, where you can switch sessions, park, recall, and more.'
4 selectable option cards in a 2x2 grid: Ctrl+B (tmux style, Recommended), Ctrl+Space (Spacemacs style), Ctrl+` (Backtick, no conflicts), Ctrl+A (screen style)
Clicking an option highlights it (selected state with border color change)
Ctrl+B is pre-selected as the default/recommended option
Bottom has step indicator (Step 1 of 4 with dots), Skip link, and Next button
Clicking Next calls config_update to write keys.prefix and advances to step 2
Closing onboarding (X button or Escape) uses default values (ctrl+b) and sets onboarding_completed = true via config_update
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Onboarding wizard — Shell selection (Step 2)
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want to choose my default shell during onboarding so that sessions start with my preferred shell.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding Step 2 title: 'Choose your default shell'
Calls detect_shells Tauri command to get available shells
Displays detected shells as selectable list items (e.g. /bin/zsh, /bin/bash, /bin/fish)
Current $SHELL value is pre-selected (matched against detect_shells results); falls back to first item if no match
Clicking a shell item highlights it as selected
Back button returns to Step 1, Skip link advances to Step 3 with default shell, Next button writes shell.default via config_update and advances to Step 3
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Onboarding wizard — Theme and Font selection (Step 3)
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want to choose a terminal theme and font during onboarding so that the app looks right from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding Step 3 title: 'Customize your terminal'
Left side: 3 preset theme cards (Dark (default), Solarized Dark, Monokai) — clicking one selects it

Each preset theme includes full ANSI 16 color definitions (background, foreground, cursor, selection + black/red/green/yellow/blue/magenta/cyan/white + 8 bright variants) defined in a presetThemes.ts file
Right side: font family dropdown (monospace, JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, Menlo) + font size slider (10-20, default 14)
Bottom preview area shows a few lines of colored sample text (ANSI escape sequences rendered as colored spans or a mini terminal) that updates in real-time when theme or font changes
Dark theme is pre-selected by default
Back button returns to Step 2, Skip link advances to Step 4 with defaults, Next button writes theme.* (all ANSI colors) and terminal.font_family + terminal.font_size via config_update
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Onboarding wizard — Completion page (Step 4) and App integration
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want to see a completion page with quick tips and then start using the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding Step 4 title: 'You're all set!'
Shows the user's chosen prefix key in a tip: 'Press [prefix key] to enter Navigation Mode. Try creating a new session with [prefix key] then N.'
Start button closes onboarding, sets onboarding_completed = true via config_update
After onboarding closes, App.svelte proceeds to create the default session and show the terminal
On subsequent launches, onboarding_completed is true so onboarding is skipped entirely
Back button returns to Step 3
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build shared ContextMenu component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable ContextMenu component so that DeckPane, ShelfCard, and TerminalPane can share the same context menu UI.
acceptanceCriteria: New ContextMenu.svelte component that accepts: position ({x, y}), items array, and onClose callback
Each item has: label (string), onClick (function), optional color (string for text color, e.g. red for Kill), optional disabled (boolean), optional type ('separator' for divider lines)
Supports a 'submenu' item type: item with children array that renders as a nested menu on hover (used for color picker)
Menu style matches existing TerminalPane context menu: #1e1e1e background, #333 border, 6px border-radius, box-shadow
Clicking outside the menu calls onClose
Menu items have hover highlight (#3b82f620 background)
Disabled items have #555 text color and no hover effect
Separator renders as a 1px #2a2a2a horizontal line
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build ColorPicker submenu component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a color picker submenu that shows 8 preset color dots so that context menus can include a Change Color option.
acceptanceCriteria: New ColorPicker.svelte component (or inline within ContextMenu submenu) that displays 8 color dots in a row: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Cyan, Blue, Purple, Pink
Each dot is a small circle (12-16px) with the corresponding color, hover shows a slightly larger size or ring
Clicking a dot calls an onSelect(colorToken) callback with the ColorToken string
Current session color is indicated (e.g. ring or checkmark on the active color)
Component can be embedded inside ContextMenu as a submenu panel
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add DeckPane title bar action buttons (focused pane only)
userStories.description: As a user, I want action buttons on the focused Deck Pane's title bar so that I can Park, Rename, or Close a session with the mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: DeckPane.svelte pane-header right side shows action buttons only when isFocused is true AND pane is hovered
Button group (left to right): Park button (Unicode pin or down-arrow icon), Rename button (pencil icon), Close button (x icon)
Park button tooltip: 'Park to Shelf' with keyboard shortcut, click calls onPark callback
Rename button tooltip: 'Rename' with keyboard shortcut, click triggers inline rename mode (existing isRenaming logic)
Close button tooltip: 'Close' with keyboard shortcut, click calls onClose callback
Buttons use simple Unicode characters or inline SVG, no icon library added
Buttons have subtle hover background highlight
Buttons do not intercept terminal click/input events (they are in the header, not terminal-container)
Peripheral panes (isFocused=false) do NOT show these buttons
App.svelte passes onPark and onClose callbacks to Deck, which passes them to DeckPane
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add DeckPane title bar right-click context menu
userStories.description: As a user, I want a right-click menu on the Deck Pane title bar so that I can access all session management options with the mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-clicking on DeckPane .drag-handle or .pane-header area opens a ContextMenu

Menu items: 'Park to Shelf' (calls session_park), 'Rename' (enters inline rename), 'Change Color' (opens ColorPicker submenu), separator, 'Close' (calls session_close), 'Kill' (calls session_kill, red text)
Right-clicking the terminal content area still shows the existing Copy/Paste/Clear menu (unchanged)
Context menu uses the shared ContextMenu.svelte component
Change Color submenu shows 8 color dots, clicking one calls session_set_color
Menu closes when clicking outside or after selecting an action
Works on both focused and peripheral panes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add ShelfCard right-click context menu
userStories.description: As a user, I want a right-click menu on Shelf Cards so that I can Recall, Rename, Kill background sessions with the mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-clicking a ShelfCard opens a ContextMenu (prevents default browser menu)

Menu items: 'Recall to Deck' (calls session_recall), 'Rename' (shows inline input or small prompt for new name, then calls session_rename), 'Change Color' (opens ColorPicker submenu with 8 dots, calls session_set_color), separator, 'Close' (calls session_close), 'Kill' (calls session_kill, red text)
Left-clicking ShelfCard still directly triggers Recall (existing behavior preserved)
Context menu uses the shared ContextMenu.svelte component
Shelf.svelte passes necessary callbacks (onRecall, onRename, onSetColor, onClose, onKill) from App.svelte
App.svelte adds handler functions for rename, set_color, close, kill on shelf sessions
Menu closes when clicking outside or after selecting an action
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Migrate TerminalPane context menu to shared ContextMenu component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want TerminalPane to use the shared ContextMenu component so that all context menus have consistent styling and behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: TerminalPane.svelte replaces its inline context menu markup with the shared ContextMenu.svelte component
Menu items remain: Copy (disabled when no selection), Paste, separator, Clear Screen
All existing behavior preserved: copy writes to clipboard, paste reads clipboard and sends to session_write, clear sends \x0c
Context menu styling is identical to before (since ContextMenu.svelte uses the same styles)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
